headline: Michigan and Boston College seek first wins from dire straits
subhead: Two ranked teams in freefall collide looking to arrest early-season collapse.

xFactor.why: BC's defense must generate pressure and limit explosive plays after surrendering 43 points to Texas.

keyMatchup: Michigan's offensive line stability against Boston College's front seven will determine whether the Wolverines can establish rhythm and control tempo.
body: Both teams arrive at this matchup having hit rock bottom in their past outings. Michigan's 24-45 loss to Oklahoma exposed serious execution issues, while Boston College's 6-43 collapse against Texas suggested defensive breakdowns at every level. The common denominator is dysfunction, and that makes this less about which team is better and more about which staff can impose its will and build confidence in a single game.

Michigan enters as the slight favorite, partly due to ranking position but also because offensive inconsistency is often easier to fix than wholesale defensive failure. Boston College needs its defensive line to hold up—if the Wolverines' ground game finds success early, the Eagles' secondary will face relentless passing situations it cannot handle. This is a game either team can win if it simply executes its basic gameplan without self-inflicted errors.
